name: ai-agent-development
description: AI agent development workflow for building autonomous agents, multi-agent systems, and agent orchestration with CrewAI, LangGraph, and custom agents.
capabilities: Design agent architecture with defined purpose, capabilities, and success metrics
Implement single agents using frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph
Build multi-agent systems with role definitions and task delegation
Create agent orchestration workflows with state management and conditional branches
Integrate tools into agents with proper error handling
Implement agent memory systems including short-term, long-term, and entity memory
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 42 potential issues (32 external_commands, 10 weak_crypto). After evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The external_commands detections are markdown backticks used for skill references (e.g., `ai-agents-architect`), not shell execution. The weak_crypto detections are keyword false positives on common words like 'design', 'memory', 'tool'. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code.
valueStatement: This workflow guides you through building autonomous AI agents, from single-agent implementation to multi-agent systems with orchestration using CrewAI and LangGraph frameworks.
userTitle: Build AI Agents with CrewAI and LangGraph
limitations: This is a workflow guide, not executable code - users must implement the actual agents
Does not include pre-built agents or templates
Requires knowledge of Python and AI/ML frameworks
Does not cover deployment or production infrastructure

useCases.targetUser: Software developers building AI-powered customer service applications
useCases.title: Build a Customer Support Agent
useCases.description: Create an autonomous agent that handles customer inquiries, accesses knowledge bases, and escalates complex issues to human agents.

useCases.targetUser: Data scientists and researchers needing automated research workflows
useCases.title: Develop Multi-Agent Research Team
useCases.description: Build a team of specialized AI agents that collaborate on research tasks, with separate agents for data gathering, analysis, and reporting.

useCases.targetUser: Engineers building conversational AI systems with memory
useCases.title: Create Stateful Agent Workflows
useCases.description: Design persistent agent workflows that maintain state across conversations and can make decisions based on workflow history.

promptTemplates.title: Start Agent Design
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to build an AI agent. Use the @ai-agent-development workflow to guide me through the process. Start with Phase 1: Agent Design. Help me define the agent purpose, capabilities, tool integration needs, and success metrics.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginning a new AI agent project

promptTemplates.title: Implement Single Agent
promptTemplates.prompt: Following the @ai-agent-development workflow Phase 2, help me implement a single autonomous agent. I want to use [CrewAI/LangGraph] as my framework. Guide me through implementing the agent logic, adding tool integration, and configuring memory.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a single agent with a specific framework

promptTemplates.title: Build Multi-Agent System
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to create a multi-agent system. Using Phase 3 of @ai-agent-development, help me define agent roles, set up communication between agents, configure task delegation, and test coordination.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a system with multiple collaborating agents

promptTemplates.title: Add Memory and Tools
promptTemplates.prompt: My agent needs memory and tools. Following Phases 5 and 6 of @ai-agent-development, help me design the memory structure (short-term, long-term, entity) and implement the tools the agent will use.
promptTemplates.scenario: Adding memory and tool capabilities to an existing agent

bestPractices: Start with clear agent purpose and defined success metrics before implementation
Use the phased workflow systematically - complete each phase before moving to the next
Test agent behavior at each phase with real-world scenarios before adding complexity
antiPatterns: Skipping the design phase and jumping directly to implementation
Adding too many tools and capabilities before verifying basic functionality
Ignoring memory system design - agents without memory lose context quickly

faq.question: What frameworks does this workflow support?
faq.answer: This workflow supports CrewAI, LangGraph, and custom agent implementations. The phases are framework-agnostic but include specific guidance for each framework.

faq.question: Do I need to know Python to use this workflow?
faq.answer: Yes, implementing AI agents typically requires Python knowledge and familiarity with AI/ML libraries. This is a technical workflow for developers.

faq.question: Can I use this for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes, this workflow provides guidance for building agents. The resulting agents can be used in commercial products subject to the licenses of the frameworks used.

faq.question: How long does it take to build an agent following this workflow?
faq.answer: Timeline varies based on agent complexity. A simple single-agent might take a few hours. Complex multi-agent systems with orchestration can take several days to weeks.

faq.question: Does this include pre-built agent code?
faq.answer: No, this is a workflow and guidance document. You will need to implement the actual agent code based on the architectural decisions made in each phase.

faq.question: What happens if my agent fails the evaluation phase?
faq.answer: The workflow includes iteration - return to earlier phases to refine agent design, improve tool integration, adjust memory systems, or modify orchestration logic based on evaluation results.
